explicit_text: I was stationed at Pearl Harbor in Hawaii in late 1941, just before the infamous attack on December 7th. Our ship, the USS California, was there along with several other battleships. I was on duty during the attack and remember the chaos as torpedoes and bombs hit the ships. We evacuated and returned multiple times, trying to save the ship. It was a terrifying experience, and I saw firsthand the destruction, including the Arizona exploding and the Nevada running aground to block the channel.

explicit_text: I served in the United States Navy during World War II, primarily in the Pacific Theater. I enlisted in 1941 from West Texas and was involved in major events like the attack on Pearl Harbor and the Philippines campaign in 1944-45. I served on several battleships, including the USS California, USS Maryland, and USS West Virginia. The war was intense, with kamikaze attacks and naval battles, but I was proud to be part of the effort that helped defeat Japan.

explicit_text: I joined the United States Navy in 1941 with two friends from West Texas because we wanted to ride instead of walk. We went to Dallas for our exams and then to San Diego for boot camp. The Navy was different from other branches; we had to buy our own uniforms and got paid more than the Army. I trained on gun mounts and served aboard battleships like the USS California and USS West Virginia. The Navy was my family during the war, and I stayed with it until the end of World War II.

It was from West Texas that my military journey began, leading me eventually to Pearl Harbor and the Pacific War.

explicit_text: When we arrived at boot camp in San Diego, we were told not to bring any clothes because whatever we had would be given to the Salvation Army. explicit_text: I served aboard the USS California before and during the attack on Pearl Harbor in 1941. We had been training on gun mounts and doing watches when the Japanese attacked. The ship took three torpedoes and a bomb hit; we evacuated but kept trying to save her. The California was sunk but remained upright, and I remember the intense fighting and efforts to keep her from rolling over like the Oklahoma. It was a harrowing experience that marked the start of my wartime service.

The Nevada was the only battleship to get underway during the attack, and its actions were vital in keeping the harbor accessible.

explicit_text: During the Pearl Harbor attack, the USS Oklahoma was hit by torpedoes and rolled over, unlike the California which stayed upright. Its fate was a grim reminder of the attack's devastation.
